free_text: Measurement of differential thick-target neutron
yields of C, Al, Ta, W(p,xn) reactions for 50-MeV
protons
Main reference. See also TITLE and AUTHOR.
Preliminary report on C,W(d,xn)
- Target is natural.
- Chemical-form of target is element.
- Physical-form of target is solid.
- Target-thickness: 15 mm (C), 10 mm (Al) and 4.0 mm
(Ta). The thicknesses of C and Ta are full-stop
thickness for 50-MeV protons.
To obtain outgoing neutron energy
Measurements were normalized by the beam
charge on the targets counted by the current
digitizer, model ORTEC-439, except for the
case of the aluminum target, which was
slightly thinner than the proton range. In
this case, the beam charge was interpolated
from runs before and after the aluminum runs.
K=110 MeV AVF cyclotron and
beam-swinger system at Cyclotron and
Radioisotope Center (CYRIC)
Two NE213 scintillation detectors. The
efficiency versus energy curves were obtained
by the calculation using SCINFUL-R [S.Meigo,
Nucl. Instrum. Methods. A401(1997)365].
The results obtained were corrected for the effects of
(a) neutron attenuation within the target, and (b)
neutron attenuation in the air and wall of the target
chamber.
Quadratic sum of statistical error, detector
efficiency, and the data correction for
neutron attenuation.
Data library LA150 are compared with experimental
spectra for C, Al and W (LA150 data are not available
for Ta). LA150 reproduces the general trend of the
experimental data fairly well but still shows marked
systematic disagreement in particular in high-energy
regions.
Thick target neutron yields in the Li(d,n) for 25 MeV
deuterons
Proofread by Masayuki Hagiwara (2004.03.27)
Tada. Secondary reference added. Reference
modified.
